blocks:
Surah At-Taghabun

Medinan, though Ibn Abbas said it is Meccan except for the last three verses. The number of its verses is eighteen verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (1) All that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth glorifies Allah. To Him belongs the sovereignty, and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things. (٢) هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ فَمِنْكُمْ كافِرٌ وَمِنْكُمْ مُؤْمِنٌ. (2) It is He who created you, then some of you are faithless and some of you are faithful. In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That He was asked about this verse, and He said: "Allah knew their faith (ايمانهم) through their acceptance of our wilayah (بولايتنا) and their disbelief (كفرهم) through their abandonment of it, on the Day He took the covenant (الميثاق) from them in the loins (صلب) of Adam while they were particles (ذرّ)." وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ. (And Allah is watchful of what you do.)

(٣) خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ ** (3) He created the heavens and the earth with truth, and He shaped you, and perfected your shapes. where He adorned you with the choicest (صفوة) qualities (أوصاف) of the creatures (الكائنات), and distinguished you with the essence (خلاصته) of the characteristics (خصائص) of the originated things (المبدعات), and made you the model (أنموذج) of all created things (المخلوقات). ** وَإِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ ** and toward Him is the ultimate return. So, perfect your inner selves (سرائركم) so that your outer selves (ظواهركم) are not touched by the punishment (العذاب).

(٤) يَعْلَمُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَيَعْلَمُ ما تُسِرُّونَ وَما تُعْلِنُونَ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ ** (4) He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and He knows whatever you keep secret and whatever you disclose, and Allah knows best what is in the breasts. So nothing is hidden from Him. (٥) أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (5) Has there not come to you the news of those who disbelieved before? Like the people of Noah, Hud, and Salih. فَذاقُوا وَبالَ أَمْرِهِمْ (then they tasted the evil consequence of their conduct)—the harm of their disbelief in the world. The origin of الوبال (al-wabāl) is heaviness (الثقل). وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (and there is a painful punishment for them) in the Hereafter. (٦) ذلِكَ بِأَنَّهُ كانَتْ تَأْتِيهِمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَقالُوا أَبَشَرٌ يَهْدُونَنا ** (6) That is because their apostles used to bring them manifest proofs, but they said, 'Shall human beings guide us?' They denied and wondered that the messengers should be human beings, and البشر (al-bashar) is applied to both the singular and the plural. فَكَفَرُوا ** (so they disbelieved) in the messengers وَتَوَلَّوْا ** (and they turned away) from reflecting upon the manifest proofs. وَاسْتَغْنَى اللهُ ** (and Allah dispensed) with everything, let alone their obedience. وَاللهُ غَنِيٌ ** (and Allah is all-sufficient) Independent of their worship and everything else, حَمِيدٌ ** (all-laudable) Whom everything praises with the tongue of its state (بلسان حاله).

(٧) زَعَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنْ لَنْ يُبْعَثُوا قُلْ بَلى وَرَبِّي لَتُبْعَثُنَّ ثُمَّ لَتُنَبَّؤُنَّ بِما عَمِلْتُمْ وَذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيرٌ. (7) The faithless claimed that they will never be resurrected. Say, 'Yes, by my Lord, you will surely be resurrected, and then you will surely be informed of what you did, and that is easy for Allah.' (you shall be resurrected) (then you will surely be informed of what you did) through accountability (بالمحاسبة) and recompense (والمجازاة). ** وَذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيرٌ.**

(٨) فَآمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** (8) So have faith in Allah and His Apostle (Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله) ** وَالنُّورِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْنا ** and the Light which We have sent down It is said that this means the Quran. And al-Qummi said: The Light (النُّورِ) means the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) عليه السلام.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The Imamate (الإمامة) is the Light (النور). And that is His Almighty saying: فَآمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالنُّورِ الَّذِي أَنْزَلْنا (So have faith in Allah and His Apostle and the Light which We have sent down). He said: The Light (النور) is the Imam (الإمام).

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: The Light (النُّورِ), by Allah, is the Imams (الأئمّة). For the light of the Imam in the hearts of the believers is brighter than the shining sun during the day. They are the ones who illuminate the hearts of the believers, and Allah screens their light from whomever He wills, so He darkens their hearts and covers them with it. And al-Qummi narrated what is similar in meaning, with an addition. ** وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ.** and Allah is well aware of what you do.

(٩) يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُكُمْ ** (9) The day He gathers you And it was read with the nūn (in the preceding context, or referring to a variant reading). لِيَوْمِ الْجَمْعِ ** (for the Day of Gathering) is because of what is in it of reckoning (الحساب) and recompense (الجزاء). And the gathering (الجمع) is the gathering of the first ones (الأوّلين) and the last ones (الآخرين). ذلِكَ يَوْمُ التَّغابُنِ ** (that is the Day of Mutual Loss and Gain.) Some of them will mutually cause loss to others therein, due to the descent of the felicitous ones (السعداء) into the stations (منازل) of the wretched ones (الأشقياء) had they been felicitous, and vice versa.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله regarding its interpretation, He said: There is no believing servant who enters Paradise except that he is shown his station (مقعده) in the Fire had he done evil, so that he may increase in gratitude (شكراً). And there is no servant who enters the Fire except that he is shown his station in Paradise had he done good, so that he may increase in regret (حسرة).

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: It is the Day when the people of Paradise cause loss to the people of the Fire. ** وَمَنْ يُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صالِحاً يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّئاتِهِ وَيُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً ** (But whoever believes in Allah and acts righteously, He will absolve him of his misdeeds and admit him into gardens with streams running in them, to abide in them forever.) And it was read with the nūn in both of them. ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. (that is the great success.)

(١٠) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ ** (10) But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs—they are the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it. And what a wretched destination! The two verses are a clarification of al-taghābun (التغابن) (mutual loss and gain) and a preference for it. (١١) ما أَصابَ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ ** (11) No affliction visits [anyone] except by Allah’s leave. except by His decree (بتقديره) and His will (ومشيئته). وَمَنْ يُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ ** And whoever believes in Allah, He guides his heart. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, he affirms Allah in his heart. So when Allah makes clear [the truth] to him, he chooses guidance, and Allah increases him [in guidance], just as He said: وَيَزِيدُ اللهُ الَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا هُدىً** (and Allah increases those who are guided in guidance).

In al-Kafi (الكافي), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, the heart (القلب) sways (ليترجّح) between the chest (الصدر) and the throat (الحنجرة) until it is bound to faith (الإيمان). When it is bound to faith, it settles (قرّ). And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَمَنْ يُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ (And whoever believes in Allah, He guides his heart)." وَاللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (11) And Allah is all-aware of all things. even the hearts (القلوب) and their states (وأحوالها).

(١٢) وَأَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ فَإِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ ** (12) And obey Allah, and obey the Apostle. But if you turn away, then there is no harm upon him, for Our Apostle is only responsible for the manifest conveyance (الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ), and he has conveyed it. (١٣) اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (13) Allah—there is no god except Him. So in Allah let the faithful put their trust. because faith in the Oneness of God (التوحيد) necessitates that. (١٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ مِنْ أَزْواجِكُمْ وَأَوْلادِكُمْ عَدُوًّا لَكُمْ ** (14) O you who have faith! Indeed, among your wives and children are enemies to you. who distract you from the obedience of Allah and dispute with you concerning matters of religion or the world (الدنيا) فَاحْذَرُوهُمْ ** (so beware of them). and do not feel safe from their harms (غوائلهم) وَإِنْ تَعْفُوا ** (But if you excuse) their sins by abandoning punishment وَتَصْفَحُوا ** (and forbear) by turning away and abandoning reproach concerning them وَتَغْفِرُوا ** (and forgive) by concealing them and preparing an excuse for them regarding them فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.) He will treat you with the like of what you treated [them with] and will bestow His favor upon you.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah: When a man intended to emigrate (الهجرة) to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, his son and wife would cling to him and say: "We implore you by Allah not to leave us and abandon us, for we will be lost after you." So, some of them would obey their family and remain. Thus, Allah warned them against their sons and wives and forbade them from obeying them. And some of them would proceed and leave them, saying: "By Allah, if you do not emigrate (تهاجروا) with me, and then Allah gathers me and you in the Abode of Emigration (دار الهجرة), I will never benefit you with anything." When Allah gathered him and them together, Allah commanded him to treat them kindly and maintain ties with them. So He said: وَإِنْ تَعْفُوا وَتَصْفَحُوا وَتَغْفِرُوا فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. (But if you excuse and forbear and forgive, then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.)

(١٥) إِنَّما أَمْوالُكُمْ وَأَوْلادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ ** (15) Your possessions and children are only a trial (a test for you) وَاللهُ عِنْدَهُ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ ** and with Allah there is a great reward. for whoever prefers the love of Allah and obedience to Him over the love of possessions and children and striving for them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He was delivering a sermon when al-Hasan and al-Husayn عليهما السلام came, wearing two red shirts, walking and stumbling. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله descended to them, took them, and placed them in His lap upon the pulpit. He said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has spoken the truth: إِنَّما أَمْوالُكُمْ وَأَوْلادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ (Your possessions and children are only a trial). I looked at these two boys walking and stumbling, and I could not be patient until I interrupted my speech and lifted them up." Then He resumed His sermon.

And in Nahj al-Balagha: Let none of you say: "O Allah, I seek refuge in You from al-fitnah (الفتنة)," because there is no one who is not encompassed by fitnah (فتنة). Rather, whoever seeks refuge, let him seek refuge from the misleading (مضلّات) trials (الفتن). For Allah, the Glorified, says: وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّما أَمْوالُكُمْ وَأَوْلادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ. (And know that your possessions and children are only a trial.)

(١٦) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ ** (16) So be wary of Allah, as much as you can, So exert your effort (جهدكم) and capacity (طاقتكم) in being wary of Him, and listen to His admonitions (مواعظه) وَأَطِيعُوا ** His commands (أوامره) وَأَنْفِقُوا ** in the ways of goodness (وجوه الخير), sincerely for His Face (خالصاً لوجهه) خَيْراً لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ ** [Meaning] an expenditure (انفاقاً) that is better for your souls, or bring forth goodness (خيراً), or let the expenditure be goodness (خيراً). And this is an emphasis (تأكيد) on the urging toward compliance (الامتثال). **وَمَنْ يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفْسِهِ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ** And those who are saved from the avarice of their souls—it is they who are the felicitous. Its interpretation has preceded.

(١٧) إِنْ تُقْرِضُوا اللهَ ** (If you lend Allah) by spending wealth in what He has commanded ** قَرْضاً حَسَناً ** (a good loan) accompanied by sincerity (إخلاص) and good disposition (طيب نفس) ** يُضاعِفْهُ لَكُمْ ** (He will multiply it for you) He will make for you ten for one, up to seven hundred and more. And it was read يضعّفه ** وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ** (and forgive you) through the blessing (ببركة) of the spending (الإنفاق) ** وَاللهُ شَكُورٌ ** (and Allah is all-thankful) He gives the abundant (الجزيل) in return for the little (بالقليل) ** حَلِيمٌ ** (all-forbearing) He does not hasten the punishment (بالعقوبة).

(١٨) عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ ** (18) Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, nothing is hidden from Him. الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ ** (the) All-mighty, the All-wise. complete in power (القدرة) and knowledge (العلم).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Sūrat al-Taghābun in an obligatory prayer (فريضة), it will be an intercessor (شفيعة) for him on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) and a just witness (شاهد عدل) before the One Who accepts its testimony (شهادتها); then it will not leave him until he enters Paradise (الجنّة).
